search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae conjunctivitis dry eye symptoms contact lens red flags PDF
site:tfosdewsreport.org dry eye diagnostic methodology report symptoms staining contact lens
site:tfosdewsreport.org dry eye triaging questions photophobia pain
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Medication selection and doses were prohibited.
Symptoms alone do not establish dry-eye diagnosis.
population_applicability: Partial: TFOS covers dry-eye diagnostic methodology and DHA covers red-eye or conjunctivitis triage; the broad workflow does not encode eye-care setting, diagnostic-test availability, or alternate diagnoses.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact for selected symptom, triage, ocular-surface, eyelid, and contact-lens documentation, not autonomous diagnosis or treatment.
UAE_applicability: DHA is direct for Dubai telehealth red-eye triage. TFOS diagnostic methodology and test implementation require UAE eye-care clinician review.
recency_verification: The official TFOS report HTML and PDF metadata and current DHA Issue 2 PDF were reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer complete TFOS diagnostic-methodology replacement or newer DHA conjunctivitis issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped dry-eye identity, grittiness, visual fluctuation, and clinician-performed ocular-surface staining context without symptom-only diagnosis.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports dry-eye symptom screening, visual symptoms, tear-film tests, fluorescein or lissamine staining, and cornea, conjunctiva, or eyelid-margin observation without inferring diagnosis from symptoms alone.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped corneal, conjunctival, eyelid, and fluorescein fields only when assessed.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-observed corneal, conjunctival, eyelid, and fluorescein-staining documentation in dry-eye evaluation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped contact-lens and screen-related differential context; treatment is not mapped.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports screen-related visual discomfort, pain or photophobia as differential context, and contact-lens-associated dry-eye context without treatment instructions.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped redness, pain, photophobia, discharge, and contact-lens red-eye history as symptom or triage context.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped trauma, chemical, vision, severe-pain, photophobia, contact-lens, corneal, and severe-redness warning fields as prompts only.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped visual-acuity and pupil findings only when assessed.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped contact-lens-care, return, worsening, vision-change, and clinician-arranged follow-up documentation without doses.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

unresolved_source_gaps: Lens-hygiene and wear-duration history, symptom duration, external eye, eye movements, red reflex, fundoscopy, visual fields, lens-fit investigation, generic management, patient questions, medication plan, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
Dry-eye diagnosis, subtype, contact-lens complication, infection, trauma, chemical injury, or corneal disease must not be inferred from symptoms alone.
Treatment, medicine choice or dose, referral urgency, and exact follow-up timing remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
